skipped.reason: False positive: CVE-2023-48795 (Terrapin SSH attack) was fixed in paramiko 3.4.0 via strict key exchange support. The repo pins paramiko>=3.4.0,<4.0 in pyproject.toml (line 91) and the lock file (requirements/base.txt line 279) resolves to paramiko==3.5.1, which is above the fix version. The OSV advisory GHSA-45x7-px36-x8w8 confirms the affected range is paramiko 2.5.0 to <3.4.0. PyPI's vulnerability API returns zero CVE-2023-48795 entries for paramiko 3.5.1.

structured_output.reasoning: CVE-2023-48795 is the Terrapin SSH prefix truncation attack. Paramiko addressed it in version 3.4.0 by implementing strict key exchange (strict kex). The repo's pyproject.toml (line 91) declares paramiko>=3.4.0,<4.0, and the lock file (requirements/base.txt line 279) pins paramiko==3.5.1 — well above the 3.4.0 fix. Verified via the OSV advisory database (GHSA-45x7-px36-x8w8) that the affected range for paramiko is 2.5.0 to <3.4.0. Also confirmed via PyPI JSON API that paramiko 3.5.1 has zero CVE-2023-48795 entries (while paramiko 3.3.2 correctly shows it). The issue description's claim that 'paramiko 3.5.1 has CVE-2023-48795' is factually incorrect — the scanner that produced this finding has an outdated or incorrect vulnerability database. The <4.0 upper bound exists because sshtunnel 0.4.0 references paramiko.DSSKey (at sshtunnel.py:1093 and :1298), which was removed in paramiko 4.0 — this is an unrelated compatibility constraint. No code changes are warranted. Findings posted as a comment on the source issue: https://github.com/michaelszhu/superset/issues/37#issuecomment-4785838683
structured_output.risk_flagged: Note: paramiko 3.5.1 does have a separate, unrelated vulnerability CVE-2026-44405 (SHA-1 algorithm in rsakey.py, fixed only in paramiko 5.0.0). Upgrading to 5.0.0 is blocked by sshtunnel 0.4.0's reference to paramiko.DSSKey (removed in paramiko 4.0). This is a separate finding and not part of this CVE-2023-48795 assessment.
